title: Radical Kenyan Cabbage Fritters
description: Funky, crunchy cabbage fritters that double‑fry to a golden crisp, packed with fermented spices and perfect for a macrobiotic snack.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine shredded cabbage, grated carrot, diced onion, grated ginger, and minced garlic.
Sprinkle in ground cumin, ground coriander, and salt; mix until the vegetables are evenly coated.
Add 1 cup of water to the mixture and mash the vegetables with the back of a spoon until a loose dough forms.
Heat oil in a deep pan to 180°C for the first fry.
Drop spoonfuls of the vegetable mixture into the hot oil and fry until golden brown, about 3 minutes.
Remove fritters with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Reduce the oil temperature to 160°C for the second fry.
Re‑fry the fritters until they are extra crisp, about 2 minutes, then drain on fresh paper towels.
Serve hot with a side of spicy tomato chutney or a simple lemon wedge.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
